title: Security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 to port 22
description: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. The control fails if the security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. (+2 related finding(s))
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. The control fails if the security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. (+2 related finding(s))
what_the_fix_does: Restricts risky inbound security-group rules on unauthorized public ports.

findings.title: Security groups should not allow unrestricted access to ports with high risk
Security groups should only allow unrestricted incoming traffic for authorized ports
Security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 to port 22

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_medium__criticality_medium maps to default recommendation 'pr_only'. Risk is medium and business criticality is medium. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.60, privilege_level=0.80, internet_exposure=1.00, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is incomplete, so the recommendation remains conservative and advisory unless policy overrides it. Current execution surfaces stay PR-only, so this recommendation remains advisory until the reviewed bundle is applied.
